what_it_does: Declares a generated surface specification for view B1 ('RHM chat panel') in area B, mapping it to journey J7, listing the UI addresses it covers, and specifying the components, lint command, and token source used to build it.
observations: The file is a generated surface-spec for view B1 in the journey-spine directory tree.
Title is 'RHM chat panel' and it is scoped to area B with status 'planned'.
It declares platforms 'desktop' and 'mobile' and represents 'RHM-chat', 'RHM-verbs', and 'RHM-config'.
home_journey is 'J7' and journeys[] contains only 'J7'; cross_journeys[] is empty.
It lists 22 ui:// addresses spanning address-help, canvas, chat, scenario-player, and settings.
build_to names components address-help, canvas, chat, scenario-player, settings, a design_lint command, and a tokens source.
The lint target is 'canvas/app/src' even though other components are listed.
symbols[] is empty; the file contains no code symbols.
standouts: The address list spans five distinct components while the surface remains tied to a single journey.
build_to explicitly couples the surface to a lint gate and a single token source.
The '_what' preamble records that addresses were built to a fixed list and must never be invented.
conventions: Follows the area/surface id naming and journey-spine directory layout.
Uses real address references rather than free-form identifiers.
Separates home journey, journeys, and cross_journeys.
Links the surface to build targets, lint, and tokens.
concerns: status is 'planned', so the surface is not yet implemented.
Addresses touch settings, canvas, and scenario-player components but cross_journeys[] is empty.
The design_lint target 'canvas/app/src' does not obviously cover all listed components.
No symbols are listed even though the address inventory is rich.
notes: A planned surface spec that anchors the RHM chat panel to journey J7 and enumerates the UI addresses and build targets it touches.
questions: Is the address list exhaustive or a minimal required set?
Is cross_journeys empty because these addresses are still owned by J7, or is it an omission?
What does 'RHM' stand for in this context?
How does the lint target 'canvas/app/src' relate to components outside canvas?
Is the tokens file consumed or generated by this surface?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to be a generated surface-spec for view B1 carrying the web of addresses; the file does exactly that by listing addresses, journey mapping, and build instructions.
apparent_themes: single source of truth for design tokens
intention_signals: It tries to define a precise, buildable boundary around the RHM chat panel surface so that code, design tokens, and lint gates can be traced back to a specific view and journey.
novelty: The address list acts as a cross-component web spanning chat, settings, canvas, and scenario-player, while the file itself remains scoped to a single surface and journey.
contribution: Provides a concrete, address-level inventory and build mapping for the B1 surface that a fused system could use to trace which UI addresses, components, lint gates, and token sources belong to the RHM chat panel.
summary_for_embedding: A generated JSON surface specification for view B1 ('RHM chat panel') in area B, scoped to journey J7, listing UI addresses, target components, lint command, and token source.
intention_for_embedding: To specify the exact UI address coverage and build targets for the RHM chat panel surface so it can be implemented and validated against the design system.
